Summary: Service design needs customer feedback
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a service design lead, I need customer feedback so that I can continually improve the portal.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Portal asks customers to leave feedback at the end of a session.

*Dependencies:* 
Clear feedback-action cycle in the design team.

*Priority*
High - This will help us identify the problems in the phase 1 workflow to fix in the next phases, and we can mitigate customer-related risks if they are clearly documented.

Summary: Senior collections wants case-wise reporting for audit
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As senior collections, I want each case to return a report with metrics so that I can draft reports for compliance.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Portal outputs _audit-trailing_ report that includes,

* Contact frequency
* Payment plans offered
* Escalation timeline
* Regulations enforced 
* Amounts handled

*Dependencies:* 
Clear escalation policies supported by rules and regulations.

*Priority*
High - Finance will reject value case if we are not compliant.

Summary: Agents want case-wise reporting for operations
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As an agent, I want a single report that breaks down each case so that I don't have to find details across trackers.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Portal outputs _operational_ report that includes promise-to-pays, statuses and contact points.

*Dependencies:* 
Rules-based way to clearly document customer details in current workflow.

*Priority*
High - to-be workflow and value case relies on the fact that agent's workload decreases.

Summary: Customers want escalations dealt with promptly
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a customer, I want a short wait time from when my case is flagged to when I’m talking to an agent so that I can pay promptly.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Agents call customers back within a day of escalation.

*Dependencies:* 
There are clear escalation policies and agents are available.

*Priority*
Medium - Customer wait-time does not affect value case or to-be workflow, but is a risk in terms of portal adoption.

Summary: Agents do not want to be overwhelmed by escalations
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As an agent, I do not want to be overwhelmed by the volume of routed cases so that I am working within my limits.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Vast majority (at least 80%) of cases aren’t escalated by the portal.

*Dependencies:* 
There are well defined rules for dealing with most cases - which can then be implemented into a system.

*Priority*
High - to-be workflow and value case rely on the fact that agent workload decreases; story features critical dependency.

Summary: Agents wants routed cases to be solvable
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As an agent, I only want the cases that I can solve routed to me so that I can do my job quickly.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Ratio of straightforward cases reaches 0.8

*Dependencies:* 
Metric exists for what cases agents are good at.

*Priority*
Medium - to-be workflow doesn’t explicitly depend on re-routing, but this could affect portal adoption and create unnecessary work for agents.

Summary: Portal users want to see eligible actions
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a portal user, I want to know my eligible actions at any point so that I can use the portal easily.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Available actions shown in portal UI.

*Dependencies:* 
Well defined scope of work for each stakeholder exists.

*Priority*
High - risk that nobody will adopt the portal if its confusing.

Summary: Agent wants customers to know reason for escalation
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a collections agent, I want customers to know why they are escalated so that we can draft a payment plan easily.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Customer receives escalation summary.

*Dependencies:* 
Clear escalation workflow.

*Priority*
Low - this does not affect value case significantly as customers might already get a sense of whats going on from account summary.

Summary: Customer wants round-the-clock access
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a customer, I want to be able to access the portal at any time so that my schedule doesn’t get in the way of repayment.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Servers are up 24/7.

*Dependencies:* 
Digital infrastructure to sustain this (AWS?)

*Priority*
Medium - bottlenecks would appear in workflow where customer traffic is too high, with the risk of agent escalations all happening at once and overwhelming them.

Summary: Compliance wants 2FA in portal
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a compliance liasion, I would like an additional layer of security in the portal so that the information in agents accounts is safe.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
The portal has 2FA.

*Dependencies:* 
Agents have a work email.

*Priority*
Low - later phases will flag abusive behaviour.

Summary: Operations wants secure verification
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a operations manager, I only want delinquents to be able to register to the portal so that our servers aren’t abused.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Delinquents given key or information only known to them to create an account.

*Dependencies:* 
Unique way to identify delinquents exists.

*Priority*
High - entire case breaks down if anyone can use it. Critical dependency.

Summary: Customer wants tailored payment plans
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a customer, I want the portal to tell me why it suggests the payment plans that it does, so that I can choose one with confidence.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Suggested payment plans must have a description linking to customer.

*Dependencies:* 
Payment plan selection is not a ‘black box’ and can be traced back to case details.

*Priority*
Medium - risk of adoption if customers lose agency and clarity. Critical dependency as we aren’t sure it can be satisfied yet.

Summary: Customer wants choice in the portal
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a customer, I want the portal to give me agency and choice so that the debt collection process remains a dignified one.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Customers must be able to choose payment plans.

*Dependencies:* 
Data available to build an algorithm, and a variety of existing plans.

*Priority*
High - risk of adoption if process feels impersonal.

Summary: Customer wants feature list
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a customer, I want to know what features aren’t available on the portal before logging in so that I can go to other points of contact.

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Does the portal have a feature list?

*Dependencies:* 
Clear portal scope and escalation cases.

*Priority*
Medium - potentially saves customer and agent time, thus improving the value case. Implementing eligible actions at each stage could make this redundant - need feedback after phase 1.

Summary: Customer should know what they owe
Creator: Matheus Soffiati
Description:
*Statement:* 
As a customer I want to know what I owe before progressing a case, so that I am financially and mentally prepared. 

*Acceptance Criteria:* 
Do customers see what they owe on the landing page?

*Dependencies:* 
Legacy system delinquency data is available and up-to-date.

*Priority*
High - risk of adoption if customers find portal burdensome. This is a critical outcome raised by many stakeholders.
